A container healthcheck, plus the fixes and tidy-ups from 5.4.1 and 5.4.2 folded into one release.

## Added

- **Container healthcheck** (#60, thanks @BaukeZwart). The image now ships a Docker `HEALTHCHECK`: BombVault probes its own `/api/health` and reports the container as healthy or unhealthy, so an auto-heal tool (Autoheal and the like) can restart it if the engine ever wedges. It runs the binary itself, so nothing extra is added to the image.

## Fixed

- **Your backup folder stays readable for an off-box copy.** BombVault runs as root and restic writes each local repo owner-only (dirs `0700`, files `0600`), which on an SMB-shared backup folder could lock a non-root user out of the whole folder. After every backup BombVault now relaxes the local repo tree to group/other readable (dirs `0755`, files `0644`, only the entries that need it, setgid preserved). The repos are encrypted, so nothing is exposed, and a folder an older version locked down is healed on the next backup.

## Changed

- **Disaster-recovery definitions now live inside each repo** (container definitions in `<repo>/def`, VM definitions in `<repo>/vm-def`) instead of separate `bombvault-defs` / `bombvault-vm-defs` folders. This keeps the backup root clean and makes a copy of a repo folder self-contained. Definitions in the old location migrate into the repo automatically on the next backup, and a restore from an older backup still finds them via a fallback.
- **The off-site DR check button reads "Re-run" once a check has passed** (shown by the green "proven off-site" badge), so it is clearly an optional re-check rather than a pending task.
